definition: US fiscal deficits at 6-7% of GDP, $2T+ annual Treasury issuance, and no credible consolidation path create structural support for elevated term premium and positive long-end stock-bond correlation. The ACM term premium has moved from ~-50bp (2020) to +50-80bp (early 2026). However, markets have partially priced this — continued correlation impact requires deficits larger than expected.
evidence: ACM term premium moved from ~-50bp (2020) to +50-80bp (early 2026)
Political economy lock: neither party will pursue deficit reduction through 2028
Both the crisis path (r>g persists) and the repression path (r<g via above-target inflation) lead to positive correlation
Only path to negative correlation is successful disinflation with fiscal consolidation — lowest-probability outcome
open_questions: Has the market already fully priced $2T+/year issuance, requiring even larger deficits to continue driving positive correlation?
If QT decelerates toward terminal balance sheet in late 2026-2027, does a primary mechanical driver of long-end supply pressure fade?
Is the supply effect on term premium marginal vs. the expectations channel?
